hyperedges.description: Telemetry input processing: collection -> parsing -> schema validation
Anomaly detection workflow: baseline computation -> triage classification with structured output

nodes.label: Parsing well-formed batches should produce correct Telemetry objects.
nodes.norm_label: parsing well-formed batches should produce correct telemetry objects.

nodes.label: A batch with exactly one node reading yields exactly one Telemetry.
nodes.norm_label: a batch with exactly one node reading yields exactly one telemetry.

nodes.norm_label: a batch with 3 node readings yields 3 telemetry objects, in order.

nodes.label: node_id, timestamp, received_at, and metrics all reflect input data.
nodes.norm_label: node_id, timestamp, received_at, and metrics all reflect input data.

nodes.label: Required-field validation and tolerance for non-essential fields.
nodes.norm_label: required-field validation and tolerance for non-essential fields.

nodes.label: Record without node_id is skipped; the valid sibling record survives.
nodes.norm_label: record without node_id is skipped; the valid sibling record survives.

nodes.label: Record without a usable timestamp is skipped and logged.
nodes.norm_label: record without a usable timestamp is skipped and logged.

nodes.label: A node reading with an empty metrics dict is valid (no metrics).
nodes.norm_label: a node reading with an empty metrics dict is valid (no metrics).

nodes.label: Unexpected extra keys in the JSON do not break parsing.
nodes.norm_label: unexpected extra keys in the json do not break parsing.

nodes.label: Metric values pass through with correct, realistic units.
nodes.norm_label: metric values pass through with correct, realistic units.

nodes.label: thermal_temp_celsius stays in Celsius (no spurious conversion).
nodes.norm_label: thermal_temp_celsius stays in celsius (no spurious conversion).

nodes.label: battery_soc_percent is treated as a percent value (0-100 nominal).
nodes.norm_label: battery_soc_percent is treated as a percent value (0-100 nominal).

nodes.label: rf_signal_strength_dbm falls within a realistic negative dBm range.
nodes.norm_label: rf_signal_strength_dbm falls within a realistic negative dbm range.

nodes.label: Timestamps must normalize consistently to UTC.
nodes.norm_label: timestamps must normalize consistently to utc.

nodes.label: A standard ISO 8601 'Z' timestamp parses into a datetime field.
nodes.norm_label: a standard iso 8601 'z' timestamp parses into a datetime field.

nodes.label: A timestamp with a +00:00 offset normalizes to the same UTC instant         as t
nodes.norm_label: a timestamp with a +00:00 offset normalizes to the same utc instant         as t

nodes.label: An unparseable timestamp string causes the record to be skipped.
nodes.norm_label: an unparseable timestamp string causes the record to be skipped.

nodes.label: Invalid metric values must be safely coerced rather than crashing.
nodes.norm_label: invalid metric values must be safely coerced rather than crashing.

nodes.label: A metric with a non-numeric string value coerces to 0.0.
nodes.norm_label: a metric with a non-numeric string value coerces to 0.0.

nodes.label: A metric with a None value coerces to 0.0.
nodes.norm_label: a metric with a none value coerces to 0.0.

nodes.label: A very large float metric value is preserved, not clamped.
nodes.norm_label: a very large float metric value is preserved, not clamped.

nodes.label: A negative metric value (e.g. dBm signal strength) is preserved.
nodes.norm_label: a negative metric value (e.g. dbm signal strength) is preserved.

nodes.label: Top-level JSON parse failures must never raise out of parse().
nodes.norm_label: top-level json parse failures must never raise out of parse().

nodes.label: Unparseable JSON text is caught; parse() returns [] without raising.
nodes.norm_label: unparseable json text is caught; parse() returns [] without raising.

nodes.label: A bare '{}' JSON object parses successfully to an empty list.
nodes.norm_label: a bare '{}' json object parses successfully to an empty list.

nodes.label: Boundary conditions: empty lists, large batches, duplicates, overflow.
nodes.norm_label: boundary conditions: empty lists, large batches, duplicates, overflow.

nodes.label: A batch with node_readings: [] returns an empty result list.
nodes.norm_label: a batch with node_readings: [] returns an empty result list.

nodes.label: A batch with 100+ node readings parses every record, no truncation.
nodes.norm_label: a batch with 100+ node readings parses every record, no truncation.

nodes.label: Two readings sharing the same node_id are both kept (no dedup).
nodes.norm_label: two readings sharing the same node_id are both kept (no dedup).

nodes.label: A metric value of 1e10 is accepted without overflow or error.
nodes.norm_label: a metric value of 1e10 is accepted without overflow or error.

nodes.label: Parsing the same input must always yield identical output.
nodes.norm_label: parsing the same input must always yield identical output.

nodes.label: Calling parse() twice with the same JSON string is deterministic.
nodes.norm_label: calling parse() twice with the same json string is deterministic.

hyperedges.description: Telemetry input processing: collection -> parsing -> schema validation
Anomaly detection workflow: baseline computation -> triage classification with structured output
